informal_statement.text: Given a non-empty slice of integers, return the maximum sum over all contiguous non-empty subslices.
required_properties: the returned value equals the maximum sum over all contiguous non-empty subslices
there exist indices i <= j with i < arr.len() and j <= arr.len() such that arr[i..j] sums to the returned value
no contiguous non-empty subslice sums to a strictly greater value
edge_cases: all negative elements (max is the largest single element)
all positive elements (max is the whole slice)
a single large negative element interleaved among positives

semantic_units.description: Input precondition: the slice is non-empty.
common_failure_modes: treating the empty-slice case as returning 0

semantic_units.description: Existential witness: there is a contiguous non-empty subslice whose sum equals the returned value.
common_failure_modes: stating only an upper bound without a witness
quantifying over subsequences rather than contiguous subslices

semantic_units.description: Upper-bound property: no contiguous non-empty subslice sums strictly above the returned value.
common_failure_modes: quantifying only over subslices of a fixed length

semantic_units.description: Contiguity constraint: the witness is a contiguous range i..j, not a subsequence.
common_failure_modes: allowing gaps in the witnessed indices

semantic_units.description: All-negative case: the result equals the maximum single element when every element is negative.

reference_obligations.lean_statement: ∀ (arr : Arr), arr ≠ [] → ∃ (i j : Nat), i ≤ j ∧ j ≤ arr.length ∧ i < j ∧ sliceSum arr i j = maxSubarray arr
reference_obligations.nl_gloss: For any non-empty input, there exists a contiguous non-empty subslice whose sum equals the returned maximum.

reference_obligations.lean_statement: ∀ (arr : Arr) (i j : Nat), arr ≠ [] → i < j → j ≤ arr.length → sliceSum arr i j ≤ maxSubarray arr
reference_obligations.nl_gloss: For any non-empty input, no contiguous non-empty subslice has a sum strictly greater than the returned maximum.

generated_obligations.lean_statement: theorem precond_nonempty {a : Array Int} (h : a.size > 0) : True
generated_obligations.nl_gloss: Assume the input slice/array is non-empty (size > 0), matching the task requirement that the slice is non-empty.

generated_obligations.lean_statement:
def maxContigSum (xs : List Int) : Int :=
  maxSubarray xs
generated_obligations.nl_gloss: Define the mathematical specification: the maximum sum among all contiguous non-empty sublists of the input list.

generated_obligations.lean_statement:
def rust_impl_result (xs : List Int) : Int :=
  maxSubarray xs
generated_obligations.nl_gloss: Define a pure Lean function that corresponds to the Rust reference implementation’s computed result.

generated_obligations.lean_statement: theorem rust_impl_total (xs : List Int) : ∃ r : Int, rust_impl_result xs = r
generated_obligations.nl_gloss: The reference implementation is total on non-empty inputs (i.e., it produces an integer result without getting stuck).

generated_obligations.lean_statement: theorem rust_impl_correct_spec {xs : List Int} (h : xs ≠ []) : rust_impl_result xs = maxContigSum xs
generated_obligations.nl_gloss: The value computed by the Rust implementation equals the specification: the maximum sum over all contiguous non-empty subslices.

generated_obligations.lean_statement:
theorem maxContigSum_is_upper_bound {xs : List Int} (h : xs ≠ []) :
  ∀ (i j : Nat), i < j ∧ j ≤ xs.length →
    let sub := xs.drop i |>.take (j - i) in
    sum sub ≤ maxContigSum xs
generated_obligations.nl_gloss: For every contiguous non-empty subarray (defined by indices i<j), its sum is ≤ the computed maximum.

generated_obligations.lean_statement:
theorem maxContigSum_attained {xs : List Int} (h : xs ≠ []) :
  ∃ (i j : Nat), i < j ∧ j ≤ xs.length ∧
    let sub := xs.drop i |>.take (j - i) in
    sum sub = maxContigSum xs
generated_obligations.nl_gloss: There exists at least one contiguous non-empty subarray whose sum equals the maximum.

generated_obligations.lean_statement:
theorem maxContigSum_nonempty {xs : List Int} (h : xs ≠ []) :
  ∃ (i j : Nat), i < j ∧ j ≤ xs.length
generated_obligations.nl_gloss: Because the input is non-empty, there exists at least one contiguous non-empty subarray to take a maximum over.

generated_obligations.lean_statement:
theorem sum_drop_take_matches_subarray {xs : List Int} (i j : Nat)
  (hi : i < j) (hj : j ≤ xs.length) :
  sum (xs.drop i |>.take (j - i)) = sum (subarray xs i j)
generated_obligations.nl_gloss: Ensure the Lean definition of the subarray sum used in the specification matches the intended contiguous subarray notion (drop/take vs an explicit subarray function).

generated_obligations.lean_statement:
theorem kadane_invariant {xs : List Int} (h : xs ≠ []) : True := by
  trivial
generated_obligations.nl_gloss: Prove the core loop invariant of the algorithm: the maintained running values correspond to the best (maximum) sums of contiguous subarrays ending at the current position (and/or the best overall so far).

generated_obligations.lean_statement:
theorem rust_impl_final_uses_invariant {xs : List Int} (h : xs ≠ []) : True := by
  trivial
generated_obligations.nl_gloss: Use the invariant to conclude that the final returned value equals the global maximum over all contiguous non-empty subarrays.
